public_goals: Have enough notice before extracurricular commitments are finalized.
Avoid feeling blindsided by activity decisions.
Use a predictable co-parent communication process.
private_concerns: Worries last-minute activity decisions create avoidable conflict.
Feels written summaries would reduce repeated misunderstandings.
Does not want to be painted as obstructive for asking for process.
red_lines: Will not accept repeated extracurricular commitments made without prior discussion.
Will not rely only on informal memory for important child-activity decisions.
emotional_triggers: important decisions left only to memory
disclosure_tendencies: becomes more specific when written-summary options are acknowledged
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: gets cooler if fairness concerns are minimized
re-engages when the process separates notice from permission control
starting_positions: Primary position: extracurricular commitments should not be finalized without enough notice to discuss them first.
Secondary position: written summaries should follow important child-activity decisions.
likely_openings: I need enough notice before new activities are locked in.
We keep re-fighting things because nothing gets written down.
